title: Buttery Silky Romantic Spanish Chinese Long Beans Stir-fry
description: This Low-Carb Friendly dish combines creamy and buttery flavors with a twist of spicy and sweet taste to create a romantic Spanish-inspired stir-fry using chinese long beans, perfect for any dinner.

ingredients.name: dash of sriracha sauce or red pepper flakes (optional, for spice)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Place chinese long beans on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Season them lightly with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven for about 20 minutes until tender but still slightly crisp.
While beans are roasting, roughly chop the garlic cloves and mince jalapeno peppers finely if using. Set aside.
In a large skillet over medium heat, melt coconut cream by microwaving gently or on stove top, stirring occasionally to avoid burning.
Add minced garlic, red bell pepper slices, and optional jalapeno peppers to the skillet. Stir fry for about 5 minutes until veggies soften slightly.
Remove roasted beans from oven and set aside. Add ground black pepper and sugar to coconut cream in the skillet, whisking well to combine flavors.
Gently fold roasted chinese long beans into the creamy mixture and continue to stir-fry for another minute or two allowing everything to meld together nicely.
Taste test and adjust seasoning if necessary with salt or sriracha/red pepper flakes (if using). Serve hot garnished with a sprinkle of ground almonds for added crunch and flavor.
